heading: Special financial assistance by the corporation
label: 1432 Special financial assistance by the corporation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The corporation shall provide special financial assistance to an eligible multiemployer plan under this section, upon the application of a plan sponsor of such a plan for such assistance.

children.heading: Inapplicability of certain repayment obligation
children.label: 2 Inapplicability of certain repayment obligation
children.text: A plan receiving special financial assistance pursuant to this section shall not be subject to repayment obligations with respect to such special financial assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: the plan is in critical and declining status (within the meaning of ) in any plan year beginning in 2020 through 2022;section 1085(b)(6) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a suspension of benefits has been approved with respect to the plan under as of ;section 1085(e)(9) of this titleMarch 11, 2021

children.label: C
children.text: in any plan year beginning in 2020 through 2022, the plan is certified by the plan actuary to be in critical status (within the meaning of ), has a modified funded percentage of less than 40 percent, and has a ratio of active to inactive participants which is less than 2 to 3; orsection 1085(b)(2) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the plan became insolvent for purposes of after , and has remained so insolvent and has not been terminated as of .section 418E of title 26December 16, 2014March 11, 2021

children.heading: Modified funded percentage
children.label: 2 Modified funded percentage
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(C), the term “modified funded percentage” means the percentage equal to a fraction the numerator of which is current value of plan assets (as defined in ) and the denominator of which is current liabilities (as defined in and ).section 1002(26) of this titlesection 431(c)(6)(D) of title 26section 1084(c)(6)(D) of this title

children.heading: Applications for special financial assistance
children.label: c Applications for special financial assistance

children.text: limit the materials required for a special financial assistance application to the minimum necessary to make a determination on the application;

specify effective dates for transfers of special financial assistance following approval of an application, based on the effective date of the supporting actuarial analysis and the date on which the application is submitted; and

provide for an alternate application for special financial assistance under this section, which may be used by a plan that has been approved for a partition under before .section 1413 of this titleMarch 11, 2021

children.heading: Temporary priority consideration of applications
children.label: d Temporary priority consideration of applications
A
children.text: the eligible multiemployer plan is insolvent or is likely to become insolvent within 5 years of ;March 11, 2021

children.label: B
children.text: the corporation projects the eligible multiemployer plan to have a present value of financial assistance payments under that exceeds $1,000,000,000 if the special financial assistance is not ordered;section 1431 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: the eligible multiemployer plan has implemented benefit suspensions under as of ; orsection 1085(e)(9) of this titleMarch 11, 2021

children.label: D
children.text: the corporation determines it appropriate based on other similar circumstances.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 1 Eligibility
children.text: For purposes of determining eligibility for special financial assistance, the corporation shall accept assumptions incorporated in a multiemployer plan’s determination that it is in critical status or critical and declining status (within the meaning of ) for certifications of plan status completed before , unless such assumptions are clearly erroneous. For certifications of plan status completed after , a plan shall determine whether it is in critical or critical and declining status for purposes of eligibility for special financial assistance by using the assumptions that the plan used in its most recently completed certification of plan status before , unless such assumptions (excluding the plan’s interest rate) are unreasonable.section 1085(b) of this titleJanuary 1, 2021December 31, 2020January 1, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: use the interest rate used by the plan in its most recently completed certification of plan status before , provided that such interest rate may not exceed the interest rate limit; andJanuary 1, 2021

children.label: B
children.text: for other assumptions, use the assumptions that the plan used in its most recently completed certification of plan status before , unless such assumptions are unreasonable.January 1, 2021

children.heading: Interest rate limit
children.label: 3 Interest rate limit
children.text: The interest rate limit for purposes of this subsection is the rate specified in (disregarding modifications made under clause (iv) of such section) for the month in which the application for special financial assistance is filed by the eligible multiemployer plan or the 3 preceding months, with such specified rate increased by 200 basis points.section 1083(h)(2)(C)(iii) of this title

children.heading: Changes in assumptions
children.label: 4 Changes in assumptions
children.text: If a plan determines that use of one or more prior assumptions is unreasonable, the plan may propose in its application to change such assumptions, provided that the plan discloses such changes in its application and describes why such assumptions are no longer reasonable. The corporation shall accept such changed assumptions unless it determines the changes are unreasonable, individually or in the aggregate. The plan may not propose a change to the interest rate otherwise required under this subsection for eligibility or financial assistance amount.

children.heading: Application deadline
children.label: f Application deadline
children.text: Any application by a plan for special financial assistance under this section shall be submitted to the corporation (and, in the case of a plan to which applies, to the Secretary of the Treasury) no later than , and any revised application for special financial assistance shall be submitted no later than .section 432(k)(1)(D) of title 26December 31, 2025December 31, 2026

children.heading: Determinations on applications
children.label: g Determinations on applications
children.text: A plan’s application for special financial assistance under this section that is timely filed in accordance with the regulations or guidance issued under subsection (c) shall be deemed approved unless the corporation notifies the plan within 120 days of the filing of the application that the application is incomplete, any proposed change or assumption is unreasonable, or the plan is not eligible under this section. Such notice shall specify the reasons the plan is ineligible for special financial assistance, any proposed change or assumption is unreasonable, or information is needed to complete the application. If a plan is denied assistance under this subsection, the plan may submit a revised application under this section. Any revised application for special financial assistance submitted by a plan shall be deemed approved unless the corporation notifies the plan within 120 days of the filing of the revised application that the application is incomplete, any proposed change or assumption is unreasonable, or the plan is not eligible under this section. Special financial assistance issued by the corporation shall be effective on a date determined by the corporation, but no later than 1 year after a plan’s special financial assistance application is approved by the corporation or deemed approved. The corporation shall not pay any special financial assistance after .September 30, 2030

children.heading: Manner of payment
children.label: h Manner of payment
children.text: The payment made by the corporation to an eligible multiemployer plan under this section shall be made as a single, lump sum payment.

children.heading: Amount and manner of special financial assistance
children.label: i Amount and manner of special financial assistance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Special financial assistance under this section shall be a transfer of funds in the amount necessary as demonstrated by the plan sponsor on the application for such special financial assistance, in accordance with the requirements described in subsection (j). Special financial assistance shall be paid to such plan as soon as practicable upon approval of the application by the corporation.

children.heading: No cap
children.label: 2 No cap
children.text: Special financial assistance granted by the corporation under this section shall not be capped by the guarantee under 1322a of this title.1So in original. Probably should be preceded by “section”.1

children.heading: Determination of amount of special financial assistance
children.label: j Determination of amount of special financial assistance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The amount of financial assistance provided to a multiemployer plan eligible for financial assistance under this section shall be such amount required for the plan to pay all benefits due during the period beginning on the date of payment of the special financial assistance payment under this section and ending on the last day of the plan year ending in 2051, with no reduction in a participant’s or beneficiary’s accrued benefit as of , except to the extent of a reduction in accordance with adopted prior to the plan’s application for special financial assistance under this section, and taking into account the reinstatement of benefits required under subsection (k).March 11, 2021section 1085(e)(8) of this title

children.heading: Projections
children.label: 2 Projections
children.text: The funding projections for purposes of this section shall be performed on a deterministic basis.

reinstates any benefits that were suspended under or in accordance with guidance issued by the Secretary of the Treasury pursuant to , effective as of the first month in which the effective date for the special financial assistance occurs, for participants and beneficiaries as of such month; andsection 1085(e)(9) of this titlesection 1426(a) of this titlesection 432(k)(1)(B) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: as a lump sum within 3 months of such effective date; or

children.label: B
children.text: in equal monthly installments over a period of 5 years, commencing within 3 months of such effective date, with no adjustment for interest.

children.heading: Restrictions on the use of special financial assistance
children.label: l Restrictions on the use of special financial assistance
children.text: Special financial assistance received under this section and any earnings thereon may be used by an eligible multiemployer plan to make benefit payments and pay plan expenses. Special financial assistance and any earnings on such assistance shall be segregated from other plan assets. Special financial assistance shall be invested by plans in investment-grade bonds or other investments as permitted by the corporation.

children.heading: Conditions on plans receiving special financial assistance
children.label: m Conditions on plans receiving special financial assistance

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The corporation, in consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury, may impose, by regulation or other guidance, reasonable conditions on an eligible multiemployer plan that receives special financial assistance relating to increases in future accrual rates and any retroactive benefit improvements, allocation of plan assets, reductions in employer contribution rates, diversion of contributions to, and allocation of expenses to, other benefit plans, and withdrawal liability.

children.label: A
children.text: any prospective reduction in plan benefits (including benefits that may be adjusted pursuant to );section 1085(e)(8) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: plan governance, including selection of, removal of, and terms of contracts with, trustees, actuaries, investment managers, and other service providers; or

children.label: C
children.text: any funding rules relating to the plan receiving special financial assistance under this section.

children.heading: Payment of premiums
children.label: 3 Payment of premiums
children.text: An eligible multiemployer plan receiving special financial assistance under this section shall continue to pay all premiums due under for participants and beneficiaries in the plan.section 1307 of this title

children.heading: Assistance not considered for certain purposes
children.label: 4 Assistance not considered for certain purposes
children.text: An eligible multiemployer plan that receives special financial assistance shall be deemed to be in critical status within the meaning of until the last plan year ending in 2051.section 1085(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Insolvent plans
children.label: 5 Insolvent plans
children.text: An eligible multiemployer plan receiving special financial assistance under this section that subsequently becomes insolvent will be subject to the current rules and guarantee for insolvent plans.

children.heading: Ineligibility for other assistance
children.label: 6 Ineligibility for other assistance
children.text: An eligible multiemployer plan that receives special financial assistance under this section is not eligible to apply for a new suspension of benefits under .section 1085(e)(9)(G) of this title

children.heading: Coordination with Secretary of the Treasury
children.label: n Coordination with Secretary of the Treasury
A
children.text: in determining whether to approve the application, the corporation shall consult with the Secretary of the Treasury regarding the plan’s proposed method of reinstating benefits, as described in the plan’s application and in accordance with guidance issued by the Secretary of the Treasury, and

children.label: B
children.text: the corporation shall consult with the Secretary of the Treasury regarding the amount of special financial assistance needed based on the projected funded status of the plan as of the last day of the plan year ending in 2051, whether the plan proposes to repay benefits over 5 years or as a lump sum, as required by subsection (k)(2), and any other relevant factors, as determined by the corporation in consultation with the Secretary of the Treasury, to ensure the amount of assistance is sufficient to meet such requirement and is sufficient to pay benefits as required in subsection (j)(1).

In the case of any plan which proposes in its application to change the assumptions used, as provided in subsection (e)(4), the corporation shall consult with the Secretary of the Treasury regarding such proposed change in assumptions.

If the corporation specifies in regulations or guidance that temporary priority consideration is available for plans which are insolvent within the meaning of or likely to become so insolvent or for plans which have suspended benefits under , or that availability is otherwise based on the funded status of the plan under , as permitted by subsection (d), the corporation shall consult with the Secretary of the Treasury regarding any granting of priority consideration to such plans.section 418E of title 26section 1085(e)(9) of this titlesection 1085 of this title
